summary: Backend-only hardening regression tested for submission_uncertain flow, recovery reconciliation, cancel safety, retry policy, and docs/source sync. Safety probes are now aligned with the official runner.
action_items: Keep docs claims limited to probe-verified behavior and keep live-trading status as BELUM SIAP LIVE until real exchange validation exists.
critical_code_review_comments: ExecutionEngine remains very large (2000+ LOC) and mixes submit/reconcile/cancel/recovery concerns, increasing future regression risk in trading safety paths.
Exchange-facing probes are largely MOCKED; good for deterministic safety regression, but not proof of real live-exchange edge-case behavior.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data. All probes used isolated temporary DATA_DIR.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Executed node scripts/run-probes.mjs successfully (includes live_submission_uncertain + cancel_submission_uncertain + live_execution_hardening). Docs checked: README/AUDIT_FORENSIK_PROMPT/REFACTOR_LOG/SESSION_CONTEXT_NEXT are aligned with source on submission_uncertain and GET-only safe retry policy. NOTE: exchange integrations in probes are MOCKED.
rca of the issue: No defect reproduced in requested scope. Verified by code-path inspection and probes: ambiguous transport failure goes to submission_uncertain (not immediate reject), automatic reconcile attempts openOrders then history for unique matches, cancelAllOrders explicitly skips unresolved submission_uncertain without exchangeOrderId, and retry logic applies to GET (public/private V2) while POST trade/cancel is not retried.
